name: Lightning_Mech

infobox.Systems: Electric Whip(Prime)Effect ()Chain damage through adjacent targets.(2 damage)Upgrade IBuilding Chain ()Chain through Grid Buildings without causing damage.Upgrade II+1 Damage ()Deals +1 damage to all affected tiles.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theBlitzkriegsquad.
sections.Strategy:
The key to using the Lightning Mech is positioning it next tobuildings, as meleeVekwill prioritize attacking buildings and Mechs adjacent to them. This will allow the Electric Whip current to flow through as many targets as possible. For this reason, it is greatly recommended to use aReactor Coreand power theBuilding Chainupgrade as soon as possible. Additionally, it's encouraged to use other Mechs to extend the circuit; in the Blitzkrieg Squad, theHook Mechhas natural Armor, which makes it an effective conductor. The current even flows through disabled Mechs.
Be wary of the objects you are ought to protect, such asDisposal Unit,Supply Train,Renfield Bombor its Prototypes. If they are part of the circuit, they will be damaged by the Whip, because they are not Buildings. The current flows in all possible directions, so if you discharge the whip at a T-junction, it will go both ways. The current doesn't flow through Mountains, but does flow through Rocks left byRock AcceleratororDiggers.
One of the best pilots for the Lightning Mech isSilica, who will allow the Lighning Mech to use Electric Whip twice in one turn once his Double Shot ability is active; with fully upgraded damage, this will allow the Mech to hit every enemy for 3 + 3 damage in one turn, which is enough damage to take out every enemy besides theScorpion LeaderandCentipede Leader. Another decent choice isRosie Rivets; since Electric Whip relies on friendly fire, she can move next to a disabled ally and revive it.
